segments.text: So these three men stopped answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
But Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, became very angry with Job for justifying himself rather than God.
He was also angry with the three friends, because they had found no way to refute Job, and yet had condemned him.
Now Elihu had waited before speaking to Job because they were older than he.
But when he saw that the three men had nothing more to say, his anger was aroused.
So Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite said:
I am young in years, and you are old; that is why I was fearful, not daring to tell you what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I thought, 'Age should speak; advanced years should teach wisdom.'
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But it is the spirit in a person, the breath of the Almighty, that gives them understanding.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: It is not only the old who are wise, not only the aged who understand what is right.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Therefore I say: Listen to me; I too will tell you what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I waited while you spoke, I listened to your reasoning; while you were searching for words,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I gave you my full attention. But not one of you has proved Job wrong; none of you has answered his arguments.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Do not say, 'We have found wisdom; let God, not a man, refute him.'
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But Job has not marshaled his words against me, and I will not answer him with your arguments.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: They are dismayed and have no more to say; words have failed them.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Must I wait, now that they are silent, now that they stand there with no reply?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I too will have my say; I too will tell what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For I am full of words, and the spirit within me compels me;
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: inside I am like bottled-up wine, like new wineskins ready to burst.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I must speak and find relief; I must open my lips and reply.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I will show no partiality, nor will I flatter anyone;
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: for if I were skilled in flattery, my Maker would soon take me away.
segments.speaker: Elihu
